result:
I’d make access to high-quality education universal—free at the point of use, lifelong, and available everywhere (including digital access and literacy).

It’s a lever that compounds: it improves health outcomes, economic mobility, civic participation, and innovation, and it reduces inequality and conflict over time. It also helps societies adapt faster to big risks (pandemics, climate change, misinformation, automation) because more people can understand problems, evaluate evidence, and learn new skills.

content.text:
I’d make access to high-quality education universal—free at the point of use, lifelong, and available everywhere (including digital access and literacy).

It’s a lever that compounds: it improves health outcomes, economic mobility, civic participation, and innovation, and it reduces inequality and conflict over time. It also helps societies adapt faster to big risks (pandemics, climate change, misinformation, automation) because more people can understand problems, evaluate evidence, and learn new skills.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
